Shrine|WD=Q135040018}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県南相馬市鹿島区上栃窪宮下35', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '福島県南相馬市原町区信田沢', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakamine-jinja}} [[JA:冠嶺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11395049}} == Japanese content == '''冠嶺神社'''（さかみねじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|南相馬市|Q642094}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 {{jalink|陸奥国|Q907389}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜式}}{{jalink|式内社}}百座のうち{{jalink|行方郡 (福島県)|Q11626139|行方郡}}にある八座の一座である。鹿島区上栃窪と原町区信田沢に鎮座する二社が主な論社とされている。 === 冠嶺神社（南相馬市鹿島区上栃窪） === {{神社 |名称=冠嶺神社（鹿島区上栃窪） |画像=[[ファイル:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|南相馬市|Q642094}}{{jalink|鹿島区|Q24895757}}上栃窪宮下35 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=冠嶺神社（鹿島区神栃窪）}} |祭神={{jalink|スクナヒコナ||少彦名命}}
{{jalink|ニニギ|Q1056017|天津彦火邇邇芸命}}
{{jalink|ホオリ|Q1051960|天津彦火々出見命}}
（伝）{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|闇淤加美神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建={{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の御代 |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名=八龍権現・八龍大明神 |例祭={{jalink|4月8日|Q2503}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=冠嶺神社|coordinates= {{coord2|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E}}}} }} === 祭神 === * 境内の由緒書によれば、{{jalink|スクナヒコナ||少彦名命}}と{{jalink|ホオリ|Q1051960|天津彦火々出見命}}を祀る。 ** 式内社顕正会によれば、現在の祭神は{{jalink|ニニギ|Q1056017|天津彦火邇邇芸命}}・天津彦火々出見命を祀るという。 ** 「奥相志」によれば、天津彦火邇邇芸命・天津彦火々出見命・{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|八龍大明神}}の三座を祀るという。 * {{jalink|ニニギ|Q1056017|天津彦火邇邇芸命}}・{{jalink|ホオリ|Q1051960|天津彦火々出見命}}の{{jalink|神体|Q327532|御神体}}はどちらも「騎馬の尊像」であるとされる。 === 由緒 === 由緒によれば、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の時代に{{jalink|ヤマトタケル|Q461258|日本武尊}}が東夷征伐の勅命を受けて奥州にやってきた。その時、地主神である{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田彦命}}より霊示があり、皇御孫尊（天津彦火邇邇芸命）を勧請して17日間祭祀を行った。陣を引いた山は四方を川に囲まれており、日本武尊は地の利を生かして夷賊を倒して無事平定を進めることができたという。 地主神である猿田彦命は道祖神であり、道陸神とも呼ばれていた。冠嶺神社が勧請された山は、道陸神の霊験があった山のため「道陸神山（現在の堂六神山）」と名付けられたという。道陸神山の南麓に天の真名井の水をもって「真野の池」という水場を設け冠嶺神の御手洗とした。真野川の名前はこの「真野の池」にちなむと伝わる。また、真野川の上流には「立石」という奇石があり、猿田彦命が降臨し鎮座した場所だと伝わる。 {{jalink|冷泉天皇|Q374125}}の時代である{{jalink|永承|Q1193549}}年間には{{jalink|鳳輦|Q11675302}}を持って天下泰平を祈る儀式が行われるなどしたが、その後{{jalink|康平|Q1193572}}年間に至る13年の間に{{jalink|前九年の役|Q1187448}}など戦乱が起きたため、神領社地は没収・荒廃し小祠が建つのみとなった。{{jalink|後堀河天皇|Q357221}}の御代、氏子の志願によって社殿を里に立て、{{jalink|寛喜|Q1194200}}2年（{{jalink|1230年|Q5412}}）{{jalink|4月8日|Q2503}}に道陸神山から現在の鎮座地へ御神体を遷座した。その後、氏子の要望により{{jalink|苕野神社|Q11616108}}（浪江町の苕野神社の分霊社である飯舘村の{{jalink|綿津見神社 (飯舘村)||綿津見神社}}）から「八龍大明神」と呼ばれる{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|闇淤加美神}}を相殿神として合祀した。そのため社号を八龍神祠とし、郡民から'''「八龍大明神」'''と呼ばれるようになったという。それ以降、三柱の祭神を奉斎するようになり、浜下りの神事も行われるようになったという。 記述によると、浜下りの神事は{{jalink|7月7日|Q2694}}に神輿を出御し、夜に角川原地区の熊木明神社に安置。明朝に現在の南海老地区の浜に降りて、天下泰平・武運長久・五穀豊穣・万民安全などを祈願し、祭典が終わると還幸したという。 現在の祭神のうちの一柱である少彦名命は、{{jalink|文政|Q1327119}}2年（{{jalink|1819年|Q6967}}）の郡内式内社調査により八龍神祠が冠嶺神社の論社とされ、文政8年（{{jalink|1825年|Q6997}}）に冠嶺神社へ改称した後に原町区信田沢の冠嶺神社から合祀されたと思われる。 === 境内 === * 社殿（拝殿・幣殿・本殿） * その他に石祠や境内社が境内に鎮座している。 * 境内の{{jalink|鎮守の森|Q11651158}}は「冠嶺神社の樹林一括」として南相馬市の天然記念物に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|title=かしまの文化財|url=http://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunnkazai/b-kashima/kabun.jsp|work= |publisher=南相馬市|date= |accessdate=2012-11-09}}。 ファイル:Torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|冠嶺神社鳥居 ファイル:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|冠嶺神社参道 ファイル:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward 2.JPG|冠嶺神社拝殿 ファイル:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|冠嶺神社本殿 ファイル:Small shrines of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|境内の石祠 {{-}} === 冠嶺神社（南相馬市原町区信田沢） === {{神社 |名称=冠嶺神社（原町区信田沢） |画像=[[ファイル:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|福島県|Q71707}}{{jalink|南相馬市|Q642094}}{{jalink|原町区|Q24892679}}信田沢戸ノ内194 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=冠嶺神社（原町区信田沢}} |祭神={{jalink|スクナヒコナ||少彦名命}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建=不詳 |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名= |例祭={{jalink|4月17日|Q2520}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=冠嶺神社|coordinates= {{coord2|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E}}}} }} === 祭神 === * {{jalink|スクナヒコナ||少彦名命}}を祀る。 === 由緒 === 創建不詳であるが、時代の流れとともに衰微し、小さな祠しか残されていなかったという。さらに、往古に起きた野火のために社殿は焼失し、その後、祠を建てて祀った。{{jalink|宝永|Q1145262}}から{{jalink|正徳 (日本)|Q1193848|正徳}}（{{jalink|1704年|Q6824}}-{{jalink|1715年|Q6884}}）の頃、祠官であった大田九太夫の家が絶えたため、羽黒派修験である山覚院が代わりに祭祀を行った。{{jalink|天明|Q1194121}}年間から住民が減り、文化年間には村里も社殿も悉く荒れ果てたという。当地の農夫が御神体を民家の小祠へと遷座し、これを隠し、長年に渡り本山派修験の大学院が祀ったという。時が経ち、この農家も絶えてしまったため、冠嶺神が村の小祠に祀られたということも忘れ去られてしまっていた。 他の村から佐藤氏という者がやってきて、冠嶺神を祀っていた農家の屋敷へ住み始めた。佐藤氏は農業を営みながら、坂嶺の山にある小祠を修理して祀っていた。{{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}12（{{jalink|1815年|Q6948}}）乙亥年、{{jalink|相馬中村藩|Q844079|相馬藩}}主である{{jalink|相馬益胤|Q8514605}}は、祠官社家と渡部美綱という者に行方郡内の式内社八座を調査し特定するよう命じた。その結果、{{jalink|多珂神社|Q11430895}}・{{jalink|鹿島御子神社|Q11677124}}・{{jalink|御刀神社|Q11488802}}・{{jalink|押雄神社}}・{{jalink|高座神社|Q11669904}}・{{jalink|日祭神社}}は特定したが、冠嶺神社と{{jalink|益多嶺神社}}はどの神社のことなのか不明だったため特定することができなかった。 {{jalink|文政|Q1327119}}2（{{jalink|1819年|Q6967}}）己卯年、再び調査命令が下される。郡官は「村に冠嶺の神を祀る小祠がある」という話を聞き調査を行うが、不審に思った村人は冠嶺神社の鎮座を否定したため、ますます冠嶺神社は歴史から忘れられてしまった。 {{jalink|安政|Q1327259}}年間になり、{{jalink|二宮尊徳|Q968964}}や二宮家が相馬へもたらした農業復興策である「興国安民法」のおかげで、相馬藩の財政再建や荒廃した農村の復興が進んだ。{{jalink|万延|Q1193862}}元年（{{jalink|1860年|Q7691}}）庚申年の春、冠嶺神社の宮祠復興が命じられ、会津の名工である匠人の上杉主殿頭が社殿造営を行った。 冠嶺神社には原町区信田沢と鹿島区上栃窪の二社の他に、鹿島区北海老に鎮座する鶏足（けいそく）神社も冠嶺神社であると称していたが、原町区信田沢の冠嶺神社のみが往古から「冠嶺神社」と称し、{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}も千年以上の歴史があるものとされたため、当社が冠嶺神社の本社であるとされた。 File:Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine at Kashima ward,Minami-soma city.JPG|村社・鶏足神社拝殿（南相馬市鹿島区北海老） === 境内 === * 社殿は{{jalink|常磐自動車道|Q1059875}}{{jalink|南相馬インターチェンジ|Q10908775}}西側の丘陵地に鎮座している。 * 参道入口には第一鳥居があり「冠嶺社」の扁額が掛けられている。そこから整備された石段を200メートルほど登ると冠嶺神社第二鳥居がある。 ファイル:View of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|冠嶺神社鎮座地遠景 ファイル:1st torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|冠嶺神社第一鳥居 ファイル:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|冠嶺神社参道入口 ファイル:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|参道 ファイル:2nd torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|冠嶺神社第二鳥居 ファイル:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|参道 ファイル:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|冠嶺神社拝殿 ファイル:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|拝殿正面部 ファイル:Carving of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|拝殿の彫刻
虹梁や蟇股の龍・鳳凰・草木、木鼻の獅子などの彫刻がされている ファイル:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|流造の冠嶺神社本殿 === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 奥相志（相馬市史4） 相馬市 1969年 * 延喜式冠嶺神社略記 氏子一同 平成6年4月 * 八社の一社・冠嶺神社 原町市教育委員会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さかみねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135040018}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県南相馬市鹿島区上栃窪宮下35', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '福島県南相馬市原町区信田沢', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakamine-jinja}} [[JA:冠嶺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11395049}} == Japanese content == '''Kanmine Shrine'' (Sakamine Shrine) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisoma City|Q642094}}. {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Engishiki}}{{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} It is one of eight troupes located in {{jalink|Namegata District (Fukushima Prefecture)|Q11626139|Namegata District}}. Two shrines located in Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, and Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward are said to be the main nonsha. === Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|270px]]
Haden of worship |Location={{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisoma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Kashima Ward|Q24895757}}Kamitochikubo Miyashita 35 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward)}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukunahikona no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsuhikohikonionigeiminoto}}
{{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}}
(den) {{jalink|Yamikamikami|Q644201|Yamitakamikami}} |Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} |Founded={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Also known as Hachiryu Gongen/Hachiryu Daimyojin |Regular Festival={{jalink|April 8th|Q2503}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * According to the history book in the precincts, {{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are enshrined here. ** According to the Shikinai-sha Kensho-kai, the current enshrined deity is {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsuhiko Hibunigei no Mikoto}} Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto. ** According to ``Okusōshi'', it is said to enshrine the three shrines of Amatsuhiko Hinaunigei no Mikoto, Amatsuhiko Hikoidemi no Mikoto, and {{jalink|Yokamijin|Q644201|Hachiryu Daimyojin}}. * Both the {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532|Goshintai}} of {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsuhiko Hidemige no Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are said to be "statues of horsemen." === History === According to historical records, during the era of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}, {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Nippon Takeru}} came to Oshu after receiving an imperial order to conquer Toi. At that time, they received a spiritual message from {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}, the landowner's god, and they solicited the Emperor's grandson (Amatsuhiko-Kainonige-no-Mikoto) and performed rituals for 17 days. The mountain where he camped was surrounded by rivers on all sides, and it is said that Nippon Takeru was able to take advantage of the geographical advantage and defeat the bandits and successfully pacify the area. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto, a landowner deity, was also known as Dosojin and Dorikujin. It is said that the mountain where Kanmine Shrine was built was named ``Dorokujinzan'' (currently Dorokujinzan) because it was the mountain where the god Dorikami had miraculous experiences. At the southern foot of Mt. Dorikujinzan, a waterhole called ``Mano Pond'' was established with water from Amano Manai and was used as a wash basin for Kanminejin. The name Mano River is said to have come from this ``Mano Pond.'' Additionally, there is a strange rock called ``Tateishi'' upstream of the Mano River, which is said to be the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto descended and was enshrined.During the {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193549}} era, which was the era of {{jalink|Emperor Reizei|Q374125}}, a ceremony was held to pray for peace in the land by holding a {{jalink|Hotori|Q11675302}}. However, during the subsequent 13 years leading up to the {{jalink|Kohei|Q1193572}} year, wars such as {{jalink|Zenunen no Eki|Q1187448}} occurred, and the shrine grounds were confiscated and fell into disrepair, with only a small shrine erected. During the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Gohorikawa|Q357221}}, a shrine was built in the village at the request of parishioners, and the object of worship was moved from Dorikushinzan to its current enshrined place in {{jalink|Hiroki|Q1194200}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1230|Q5412}}) {{jalink|April 8th|Q2503}}. After that, at the request of the parishioners, {{jalink|Kenno Shrine|Q11616108}} ({{jalink|Watatsumi Shrine in Iitate Village, which is a branch shrine of Kakeno Shrine in Namie Town) (Iitate Village||Watatsumi Shrine}}), {{jalink|Yamyojin|Q644201|Yamiyoshikami}}, called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'', was enshrined together as a side shrine. For this reason, the company name was changed to Hachiryu Shrine, and it came to be called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'' by the people of the county. Since then, it has been dedicated to three deities, and rituals such as riding down the beach have also started to be held. According to the description, a mikoshi is carried out for the Hamakudari ritual on {{jalink|July 7|Q2694}}, and is enshrined at Kumaki Akira Shrine in the Kadokawara area at night. It is said that in the morning of the next morning, people descended on the beach in the present-day Minami-ebi area to pray for peace and peace in the world, long life for the military, abundant harvests, and safety for all people.Sukuhikona no Mikoto, one of the current deities enshrined, was discovered according to a Shikinai shrine survey in the county in {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}). Hachiryu Shrine is thought to be the Ronsha of Kanmine Shrine, and after the name was changed to Kanmine Shrine in 1825 (Bunsei 8), it is thought to have been enshrined together with Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward. === Precincts === * Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, main hall) * In addition, there are stone shrines and precinct shrines located within the precincts. * The {{jalink|Chinju-no-Mori|Q11651158}} within the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Minamisoma City as the ``Kanmine Shrine Forest''{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Kashima's Cultural Properties|url=http://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunnkazai/b-kashima/kabun.jsp|work= |publisher=Minamisoma City|date= |accessdate=2012-11-09}}. File:Torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine File:Small shrines of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts {{-}} === Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisoma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}194 Shinoda Sawatonouchi |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} |Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} | Founded = unknown |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Regular Festival={{jalink|April 17th|Q2520}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukahikona no Mikoto}} is enshrined here. === History === Its founding is unknown, but it is said to have declined with the passage of time, and only a small shrine remained. Furthermore, the shrine building was destroyed by a wild fire in ancient times, and after that a shrine was built to enshrine it. {{jalink|Hoei|Q1145262}} from {{jalink|Mashinori (Japan)|Q1193848|Mashinori}} ({{jalink|1704|Q6824}}-{{jalink|1715|Q6884}}) When the family of Kudayu Ota, who was a shrine official, died out, Sankaku-in, a Haguro-ha Shugen, performed the rituals in their place. It is said that the number of residents decreased from the {{jalink|Tenmei|Q1194121}} era, and by the Bunka era, both the village and the shrine were in ruins. It is said that a local farmer moved the object of worship to a small shrine in a private house, hid it, and for many years it was enshrined by a graduate school of Honzan-ha Shugen Shugen. As time passed and these farmers disappeared, the fact that Kanmine no Kami was once enshrined in a small shrine in the village was forgotten.A man called the Sato clan came from another village and began living in the mansion of a farmer who was worshiping the Kanmine deity. While Mr. Sato was engaged in farming, he repaired and worshiped a small shrine on the mountain of Sakamine. {{jalink|Culture (Era name)|Q1373146|Culture}}12 ({{jalink|1815|Q6948}}) Year of the Pig, {{jalink|Soma Nakamura Domain|Q844079|Ao {{jalink|Masutane Souma|Q8514605}}, the lord of the horse clan, ordered the shrine priest and a person named Mitsuna Watabe to investigate and identify eight Shikinai shrines in Namegata district. As a result, {{jalink|Taka Shrine|Q11430895}}, {{jalink|Kashima Miko Shrine|Q11677124}}, {{jalink|Mito Shrine|Q11488802}}, {{jalink|Oshio Shrine}}, {{jalink|Koza Shrine|Q11669904}} and {{jalink|Hisatsu Shrine}} were identified, but Kanmine Shrine and {{jalink|Masutamine Shrine}} could not be identified because it was unclear which shrines they were referring to. {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}) In the year of the Rabbit, another investigation order is given. The county official heard that there was a small shrine dedicated to the god of Kanmine in the village and investigated, but the villagers, who became suspicious, denied the existence of Kanmine Shrine, and as a result, Kanmine Shrine was even more forgotten from history.In the {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}} years, thanks to {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}, the financial reconstruction of the Soma clan and the reconstruction of devastated farming villages progressed thanks to the ``Kokoku Anminho'', an agricultural reconstruction measure brought to Soma by {{jalink|Sontoku Ninomiya|Q968964}} and the Ninomiya family. {{jalink|Mannobu|Q1193862}} In the spring of the first year of the Koshin year ({{jalink|1860|Q7691}}), the restoration of the shrine shrine of Kanmine Shrine was ordered, and master craftsman Uesugi Shudono, a master craftsman from Aizu, constructed the shrine. In addition to the two Kanmine Shrines in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, and Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Keisoku Shrine, located in Kitaebi, Kashima Ward, also called itself Kanmine Shrine, but Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward It has been called Kanmine Shrine since ancient times, and the {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} is said to have a history of more than a thousand years, so our company was considered to be the headquarters of Kanmine Shrine. File:Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine at Kashima ward,Minami-soma city.JPG|Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine (Kitaebi, Kashima-ku, Minami-soma city) === Precincts === * The shrine building is located on the hill on the west side of {{jalink|Tokiwa Expressway|Q1059875}}{{jalink|Minamisoma Interchange|Q10908775}}. * At the entrance to the shrine is the first torii gate with a plaque reading ``Kanreisha.'' From there, climb up the stone steps for about 200 meters to reach the second torii gate of Kanmine Shrine. File:View of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Distant view of Kanmine-jinja shrine enshrined place File:1st torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine First Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach entrance File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Sando File:2nd torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine second torii gate File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Sando File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Front part of the shrine File:Carving of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Carving of the worship hall
There are carvings of rainbow beams, toaded dragons, phoenixes, plants, wood-nosed lions, etc. File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine main hall === Source === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Oku Soshi (Soma City History 4) Soma City 1969 * Engishiki Kanmine Shrine abbreviation All parishioners April 1994 * Kanmine Shrine, one of the eight shrines Haramachi City Board of Education {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakamine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{'text': '福島県南相馬市原町区信田沢', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakamine-jinja}} [[JA:冠嶺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11395049}} == Japanese content == '''Kanmine Shrine'' (Sakamine Shrine) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisoma City|Q642094}}. {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Engishiki}}{{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} It is one of eight troupes located in {{jalink|Namegata District (Fukushima Prefecture)|Q11626139|Namegata District}}. Two shrines located in Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, and Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward are said to be the main nonsha. === Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|270px]]
Haden of worship |Location={{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisoma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Kashima Ward|Q24895757}}Kamitochikubo Miyashita 35 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward)}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukunahikona no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsuhikohikonionigeiminoto}}
{{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}}
(den) {{jalink|Yamikamikami|Q644201|Yamitakamikami}} |Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} |Founded={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Also known as Hachiryu Gongen/Hachiryu Daimyojin |Regular Festival={{jalink|April 8th|Q2503}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * According to the history book in the precincts, {{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are enshrined here. ** According to the Shikinai-sha Kensho-kai, the current enshrined deity is {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsuhiko Hibunigei no Mikoto}} Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto. ** According to ``Okusōshi'', it is said to enshrine the three shrines of Amatsuhiko Hinaunigei no Mikoto, Amatsuhiko Hikoidemi no Mikoto, and {{jalink|Yokamijin|Q644201|Hachiryu Daimyojin}}. * Both the {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532|Goshintai}} of {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsuhiko Hidemige no Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are said to be "statues of horsemen." === History === According to historical records, during the era of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}, {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Nippon Takeru}} came to Oshu after receiving an imperial order to conquer Toi. At that time, they received a spiritual message from {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}, the landowner's god, and they solicited the Emperor's grandson (Amatsuhiko-Kainonige-no-Mikoto) and performed rituals for 17 days. The mountain where he camped was surrounded by rivers on all sides, and it is said that Nippon Takeru was able to take advantage of the geographical advantage and defeat the bandits and successfully pacify the area. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto, a landowner deity, was also known as Dosojin and Dorikujin. It is said that the mountain where Kanmine Shrine was built was named ``Dorokujinzan'' (currently Dorokujinzan) because it was the mountain where the god Dorikami had miraculous experiences. At the southern foot of Mt. Dorikujinzan, a waterhole called ``Mano Pond'' was established with water from Amano Manai and was used as a wash basin for Kanminejin. The name Mano River is said to have come from this ``Mano Pond.'' Additionally, there is a strange rock called ``Tateishi'' upstream of the Mano River, which is said to be the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto descended and was enshrined.During the {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193549}} era, which was the era of {{jalink|Emperor Reizei|Q374125}}, a ceremony was held to pray for peace in the land by holding a {{jalink|Hotori|Q11675302}}. However, during the subsequent 13 years leading up to the {{jalink|Kohei|Q1193572}} year, wars such as {{jalink|Zenunen no Eki|Q1187448}} occurred, and the shrine grounds were confiscated and fell into disrepair, with only a small shrine erected. During the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Gohorikawa|Q357221}}, a shrine was built in the village at the request of parishioners, and the object of worship was moved from Dorikushinzan to its current enshrined place in {{jalink|Hiroki|Q1194200}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1230|Q5412}}) {{jalink|April 8th|Q2503}}. After that, at the request of the parishioners, {{jalink|Kenno Shrine|Q11616108}} ({{jalink|Watatsumi Shrine in Iitate Village, which is a branch shrine of Kakeno Shrine in Namie Town) (Iitate Village||Watatsumi Shrine}}), {{jalink|Yamyojin|Q644201|Yamiyoshikami}}, called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'', was enshrined together as a side shrine. For this reason, the company name was changed to Hachiryu Shrine, and it came to be called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'' by the people of the county. Since then, it has been dedicated to three deities, and rituals such as riding down the beach have also started to be held. According to the description, a mikoshi is carried out for the Hamakudari ritual on {{jalink|July 7|Q2694}}, and is enshrined at Kumaki Akira Shrine in the Kadokawara area at night. It is said that in the morning of the next morning, people descended on the beach in the present-day Minami-ebi area to pray for peace and peace in the world, long life for the military, abundant harvests, and safety for all people.Sukuhikona no Mikoto, one of the current deities enshrined, was discovered according to a Shikinai shrine survey in the county in {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}). Hachiryu Shrine is thought to be the Ronsha of Kanmine Shrine, and after the name was changed to Kanmine Shrine in 1825 (Bunsei 8), it is thought to have been enshrined together with Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward. === Precincts === * Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, main hall) * In addition, there are stone shrines and precinct shrines located within the precincts. * The {{jalink|Chinju-no-Mori|Q11651158}} within the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Minamisoma City as the ``Kanmine Shrine Forest''{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Kashima's Cultural Properties|url=http://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunnkazai/b-kashima/kabun.jsp|work= |publisher=Minamisoma City|date= |accessdate=2012-11-09}}. File:Torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine File:Small shrines of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts {{-}} === Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisoma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}194 Shinoda Sawatonouchi |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} |Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} | Founded = unknown |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Regular Festival={{jalink|April 17th|Q2520}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukahikona no Mikoto}} is enshrined here. === History === Its founding is unknown, but it is said to have declined with the passage of time, and only a small shrine remained. Furthermore, the shrine building was destroyed by a wild fire in ancient times, and after that a shrine was built to enshrine it. {{jalink|Hoei|Q1145262}} from {{jalink|Mashinori (Japan)|Q1193848|Mashinori}} ({{jalink|1704|Q6824}}-{{jalink|1715|Q6884}}) When the family of Kudayu Ota, who was a shrine official, died out, Sankaku-in, a Haguro-ha Shugen, performed the rituals in their place. It is said that the number of residents decreased from the {{jalink|Tenmei|Q1194121}} era, and by the Bunka era, both the village and the shrine were in ruins. It is said that a local farmer moved the object of worship to a small shrine in a private house, hid it, and for many years it was enshrined by a graduate school of Honzan-ha Shugen Shugen. As time passed and these farmers disappeared, the fact that Kanmine no Kami was once enshrined in a small shrine in the village was forgotten.A man called the Sato clan came from another village and began living in the mansion of a farmer who was worshiping the Kanmine deity. While Mr. Sato was engaged in farming, he repaired and worshiped a small shrine on the mountain of Sakamine. {{jalink|Culture (Era name)|Q1373146|Culture}}12 ({{jalink|1815|Q6948}}) Year of the Pig, {{jalink|Soma Nakamura Domain|Q844079|Ao {{jalink|Masutane Souma|Q8514605}}, the lord of the horse clan, ordered the shrine priest and a person named Mitsuna Watabe to investigate and identify eight Shikinai shrines in Namegata district. As a result, {{jalink|Taka Shrine|Q11430895}}, {{jalink|Kashima Miko Shrine|Q11677124}}, {{jalink|Mito Shrine|Q11488802}}, {{jalink|Oshio Shrine}}, {{jalink|Koza Shrine|Q11669904}} and {{jalink|Hisatsu Shrine}} were identified, but Kanmine Shrine and {{jalink|Masutamine Shrine}} could not be identified because it was unclear which shrines they were referring to. {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}) In the year of the Rabbit, another investigation order is given. The county official heard that there was a small shrine dedicated to the god of Kanmine in the village and investigated, but the villagers, who became suspicious, denied the existence of Kanmine Shrine, and as a result, Kanmine Shrine was even more forgotten from history.In the {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}} years, thanks to {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}, the financial reconstruction of the Soma clan and the reconstruction of devastated farming villages progressed thanks to the ``Kokoku Anminho'', an agricultural reconstruction measure brought to Soma by {{jalink|Sontoku Ninomiya|Q968964}} and the Ninomiya family. {{jalink|Mannobu|Q1193862}} In the spring of the first year of the Koshin year ({{jalink|1860|Q7691}}), the restoration of the shrine shrine of Kanmine Shrine was ordered, and master craftsman Uesugi Shudono, a master craftsman from Aizu, constructed the shrine. In addition to the two Kanmine Shrines in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, and Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Keisoku Shrine, located in Kitaebi, Kashima Ward, also called itself Kanmine Shrine, but Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward It has been called Kanmine Shrine since ancient times, and the {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} is said to have a history of more than a thousand years, so our company was considered to be the headquarters of Kanmine Shrine. File:Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine at Kashima ward,Minami-soma city.JPG|Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine (Kitaebi, Kashima-ku, Minami-soma city) === Precincts === * The shrine building is located on the hill on the west side of {{jalink|Tokiwa Expressway|Q1059875}}{{jalink|Minamisoma Interchange|Q10908775}}. * At the entrance to the shrine is the first torii gate with a plaque reading ``Kanreisha.'' From there, climb up the stone steps for about 200 meters to reach the second torii gate of Kanmine Shrine. File:View of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Distant view of Kanmine-jinja shrine enshrined place File:1st torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine First Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach entrance File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Sando File:2nd torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine second torii gate File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Sando File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Front part of the shrine File:Carving of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Carving of the worship hall
There are carvings of rainbow beams, toaded dragons, phoenixes, plants, wood-nosed lions, etc. File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine main hall === Source === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Oku Soshi (Soma City History 4) Soma City 1969 * Engishiki Kanmine Shrine abbreviation All parishioners April 1994 * Kanmine Shrine, one of the eight shrines Haramachi City Board of Education {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakamine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Engishiki}}{{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} It is one of eight troupes located in {{jalink|Namegata District (Fukushima Prefecture)|Q11626139|Namegata District}}. Two shrines located in Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, and Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward are said to be the main nonsha. === Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|270px]]
Haden of worship |Location={{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisoma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Kashima Ward|Q24895757}}Kamitochikubo Miyashita 35 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward)}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukunahikona no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsuhikohikonionigeiminoto}}
{{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}}
(den) {{jalink|Yamikamikami|Q644201|Yamitakamikami}} |Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} |Founded={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Also known as Hachiryu Gongen/Hachiryu Daimyojin |Regular Festival={{jalink|April 8th|Q2503}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * According to the history book in the precincts, {{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are enshrined here. ** According to the Shikinai-sha Kensho-kai, the current enshrined deity is {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsuhiko Hibunigei no Mikoto}} Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto. ** According to ``Okusōshi'', it is said to enshrine the three shrines of Amatsuhiko Hinaunigei no Mikoto, Amatsuhiko Hikoidemi no Mikoto, and {{jalink|Yokamijin|Q644201|Hachiryu Daimyojin}}. * Both the {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532|Goshintai}} of {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsuhiko Hidemige no Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are said to be "statues of horsemen." === History === According to historical records, during the era of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}, {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Nippon Takeru}} came to Oshu after receiving an imperial order to conquer Toi. At that time, they received a spiritual message from {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}, the landowner's god, and they solicited the Emperor's grandson (Amatsuhiko-Kainonige-no-Mikoto) and performed rituals for 17 days. The mountain where he camped was surrounded by rivers on all sides, and it is said that Nippon Takeru was able to take advantage of the geographical advantage and defeat the bandits and successfully pacify the area. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto, a landowner deity, was also known as Dosojin and Dorikujin. It is said that the mountain where Kanmine Shrine was built was named ``Dorokujinzan'' (currently Dorokujinzan) because it was the mountain where the god Dorikami had miraculous experiences. At the southern foot of Mt. Dorikujinzan, a waterhole called ``Mano Pond'' was established with water from Amano Manai and was used as a wash basin for Kanminejin. The name Mano River is said to have come from this ``Mano Pond.'' Additionally, there is a strange rock called ``Tateishi'' upstream of the Mano River, which is said to be the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto descended and was enshrined.During the {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193549}} era, which was the era of {{jalink|Emperor Reizei|Q374125}}, a ceremony was held to pray for peace in the land by holding a {{jalink|Hotori|Q11675302}}. However, during the subsequent 13 years leading up to the {{jalink|Kohei|Q1193572}} year, wars such as {{jalink|Zenunen no Eki|Q1187448}} occurred, and the shrine grounds were confiscated and fell into disrepair, with only a small shrine erected. During the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Gohorikawa|Q357221}}, a shrine was built in the village at the request of parishioners, and the object of worship was moved from Dorikushinzan to its current enshrined place in {{jalink|Hiroki|Q1194200}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1230|Q5412}}) {{jalink|April 8th|Q2503}}. After that, at the request of the parishioners, {{jalink|Kenno Shrine|Q11616108}} ({{jalink|Watatsumi Shrine in Iitate Village, which is a branch shrine of Kakeno Shrine in Namie Town) (Iitate Village||Watatsumi Shrine}}), {{jalink|Yamyojin|Q644201|Yamiyoshikami}}, called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'', was enshrined together as a side shrine. For this reason, the company name was changed to Hachiryu Shrine, and it came to be called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'' by the people of the county. Since then, it has been dedicated to three deities, and rituals such as riding down the beach have also started to be held. According to the description, a mikoshi is carried out for the Hamakudari ritual on {{jalink|July 7|Q2694}}, and is enshrined at Kumaki Akira Shrine in the Kadokawara area at night. It is said that in the morning of the next morning, people descended on the beach in the present-day Minami-ebi area to pray for peace and peace in the world, long life for the military, abundant harvests, and safety for all people.Sukuhikona no Mikoto, one of the current deities enshrined, was discovered according to a Shikinai shrine survey in the county in {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}). Hachiryu Shrine is thought to be the Ronsha of Kanmine Shrine, and after the name was changed to Kanmine Shrine in 1825 (Bunsei 8), it is thought to have been enshrined together with Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward. === Precincts === * Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, main hall) * In addition, there are stone shrines and precinct shrines located within the precincts. * The {{jalink|Chinju-no-Mori|Q11651158}} within the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Minamisoma City as the ``Kanmine Shrine Forest''{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Kashima's Cultural Properties|url=http://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunnkazai/b-kashima/kabun.jsp|work= |publisher=Minamisoma City|date= |accessdate=2012-11-09}}. File:Torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine File:Small shrines of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts {{-}} === Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisoma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}194 Shinoda Sawatonouchi |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} |Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} | Founded = unknown |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Regular Festival={{jalink|April 17th|Q2520}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukahikona no Mikoto}} is enshrined here. === History === Its founding is unknown, but it is said to have declined with the passage of time, and only a small shrine remained. Furthermore, the shrine building was destroyed by a wild fire in ancient times, and after that a shrine was built to enshrine it. {{jalink|Hoei|Q1145262}} from {{jalink|Mashinori (Japan)|Q1193848|Mashinori}} ({{jalink|1704|Q6824}}-{{jalink|1715|Q6884}}) When the family of Kudayu Ota, who was a shrine official, died out, Sankaku-in, a Haguro-ha Shugen, performed the rituals in their place. It is said that the number of residents decreased from the {{jalink|Tenmei|Q1194121}} era, and by the Bunka era, both the village and the shrine were in ruins. It is said that a local farmer moved the object of worship to a small shrine in a private house, hid it, and for many years it was enshrined by a graduate school of Honzan-ha Shugen Shugen. As time passed and these farmers disappeared, the fact that Kanmine no Kami was once enshrined in a small shrine in the village was forgotten.A man called the Sato clan came from another village and began living in the mansion of a farmer who was worshiping the Kanmine deity. While Mr. Sato was engaged in farming, he repaired and worshiped a small shrine on the mountain of Sakamine. {{jalink|Culture (Era name)|Q1373146|Culture}}12 ({{jalink|1815|Q6948}}) Year of the Pig, {{jalink|Soma Nakamura Domain|Q844079|Ao {{jalink|Masutane Souma|Q8514605}}, the lord of the horse clan, ordered the shrine priest and a person named Mitsuna Watabe to investigate and identify eight Shikinai shrines in Namegata district. As a result, {{jalink|Taka Shrine|Q11430895}}, {{jalink|Kashima Miko Shrine|Q11677124}}, {{jalink|Mito Shrine|Q11488802}}, {{jalink|Oshio Shrine}}, {{jalink|Koza Shrine|Q11669904}} and {{jalink|Hisatsu Shrine}} were identified, but Kanmine Shrine and {{jalink|Masutamine Shrine}} could not be identified because it was unclear which shrines they were referring to. {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}) In the year of the Rabbit, another investigation order is given. The county official heard that there was a small shrine dedicated to the god of Kanmine in the village and investigated, but the villagers, who became suspicious, denied the existence of Kanmine Shrine, and as a result, Kanmine Shrine was even more forgotten from history.In the {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}} years, thanks to {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}, the financial reconstruction of the Soma clan and the reconstruction of devastated farming villages progressed thanks to the ``Kokoku Anminho'', an agricultural reconstruction measure brought to Soma by {{jalink|Sontoku Ninomiya|Q968964}} and the Ninomiya family. {{jalink|Mannobu|Q1193862}} In the spring of the first year of the Koshin year ({{jalink|1860|Q7691}}), the restoration of the shrine shrine of Kanmine Shrine was ordered, and master craftsman Uesugi Shudono, a master craftsman from Aizu, constructed the shrine. In addition to the two Kanmine Shrines in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, and Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Keisoku Shrine, located in Kitaebi, Kashima Ward, also called itself Kanmine Shrine, but Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward It has been called Kanmine Shrine since ancient times, and the {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} is said to have a history of more than a thousand years, so our company was considered to be the headquarters of Kanmine Shrine. File:Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine at Kashima ward,Minami-soma city.JPG|Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine (Kitaebi, Kashima-ku, Minami-soma city) === Precincts === * The shrine building is located on the hill on the west side of {{jalink|Tokiwa Expressway|Q1059875}}{{jalink|Minamisoma Interchange|Q10908775}}. * At the entrance to the shrine is the first torii gate with a plaque reading ``Kanreisha.'' From there, climb up the stone steps for about 200 meters to reach the second torii gate of Kanmine Shrine. File:View of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Distant view of Kanmine-jinja shrine enshrined place File:1st torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine First Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach entrance File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Sando File:2nd torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine second torii gate File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Sando File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Front part of the shrine File:Carving of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Carving of the worship hall
There are carvings of rainbow beams, toaded dragons, phoenixes, plants, wood-nosed lions, etc. File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine main hall === Source === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Oku Soshi (Soma City History 4) Soma City 1969 * Engishiki Kanmine Shrine abbreviation All parishioners April 1994 * Kanmine Shrine, one of the eight shrines Haramachi City Board of Education {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakamine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

However, during the subsequent 13 years leading up to the {{jalink|Kohei|Q1193572}} year, wars such as {{jalink|Zenunen no Eki|Q1187448}} occurred, and the shrine grounds were confiscated and fell into disrepair, with only a small shrine erected. During the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Gohorikawa|Q357221}}, a shrine was built in the village at the request of parishioners, and the object of worship was moved from Dorikushinzan to its current enshrined place in {{jalink|Hiroki|Q1194200}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1230|Q5412}}) {{jalink|April 8th|Q2503}}. After that, at the request of the parishioners, {{jalink|Kenno Shrine|Q11616108}} ({{jalink|Watatsumi Shrine in Iitate Village, which is a branch shrine of Kakeno Shrine in Namie Town) (Iitate Village||Watatsumi Shrine}}), {{jalink|Yamyojin|Q644201|Yamiyoshikami}}, called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'', was enshrined together as a side shrine. For this reason, the company name was changed to Hachiryu Shrine, and it came to be called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'' by the people of the county. Since then, it has been dedicated to three deities, and rituals such as riding down the beach have also started to be held. According to the description, a mikoshi is carried out for the Hamakudari ritual on {{jalink|July 7|Q2694}}, and is enshrined at Kumaki Akira Shrine in the Kadokawara area at night. It is said that in the morning of the next morning, people descended on the beach in the present-day Minami-ebi area to pray for peace and peace in the world, long life for the military, abundant harvests, and safety for all people.Sukuhikona no Mikoto, one of the current deities enshrined, was discovered according to a Shikinai shrine survey in the county in {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}). Hachiryu Shrine is thought to be the Ronsha of Kanmine Shrine, and after the name was changed to Kanmine Shrine in 1825 (Bunsei 8), it is thought to have been enshrined together with Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward. === Precincts === * Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, main hall) * In addition, there are stone shrines and precinct shrines located within the precincts. * The {{jalink|Chinju-no-Mori|Q11651158}} within the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Minamisoma City as the ``Kanmine Shrine Forest''{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Kashima's Cultural Properties|url=http://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunnkazai/b-kashima/kabun.jsp|work= |publisher=Minamisoma City|date= |accessdate=2012-11-09}}. File:Torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine File:Small shrines of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts {{-}} === Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisoma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}194 Shinoda Sawatonouchi |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} |Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} | Founded = unknown |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Regular Festival={{jalink|April 17th|Q2520}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukahikona no Mikoto}} is enshrined here. === History === Its founding is unknown, but it is said to have declined with the passage of time, and only a small shrine remained. Furthermore, the shrine building was destroyed by a wild fire in ancient times, and after that a shrine was built to enshrine it. {{jalink|Hoei|Q1145262}} from {{jalink|Mashinori (Japan)|Q1193848|Mashinori}} ({{jalink|1704|Q6824}}-{{jalink|1715|Q6884}}) When the family of Kudayu Ota, who was a shrine official, died out, Sankaku-in, a Haguro-ha Shugen, performed the rituals in their place. It is said that the number of residents decreased from the {{jalink|Tenmei|Q1194121}} era, and by the Bunka era, both the village and the shrine were in ruins. It is said that a local farmer moved the object of worship to a small shrine in a private house, hid it, and for many years it was enshrined by a graduate school of Honzan-ha Shugen Shugen. As time passed and these farmers disappeared, the fact that Kanmine no Kami was once enshrined in a small shrine in the village was forgotten.A man called the Sato clan came from another village and began living in the mansion of a farmer who was worshiping the Kanmine deity. While Mr. Sato was engaged in farming, he repaired and worshiped a small shrine on the mountain of Sakamine. {{jalink|Culture (Era name)|Q1373146|Culture}}12 ({{jalink|1815|Q6948}}) Year of the Pig, {{jalink|Soma Nakamura Domain|Q844079|Ao {{jalink|Masutane Souma|Q8514605}}, the lord of the horse clan, ordered the shrine priest and a person named Mitsuna Watabe to investigate and identify eight Shikinai shrines in Namegata district. As a result, {{jalink|Taka Shrine|Q11430895}}, {{jalink|Kashima Miko Shrine|Q11677124}}, {{jalink|Mito Shrine|Q11488802}}, {{jalink|Oshio Shrine}}, {{jalink|Koza Shrine|Q11669904}} and {{jalink|Hisatsu Shrine}} were identified, but Kanmine Shrine and {{jalink|Masutamine Shrine}} could not be identified because it was unclear which shrines they were referring to. {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}) In the year of the Rabbit, another investigation order is given. The county official heard that there was a small shrine dedicated to the god of Kanmine in the village and investigated, but the villagers, who became suspicious, denied the existence of Kanmine Shrine, and as a result, Kanmine Shrine was even more forgotten from history.In the {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}} years, thanks to {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}, the financial reconstruction of the Soma clan and the reconstruction of devastated farming villages progressed thanks to the ``Kokoku Anminho'', an agricultural reconstruction measure brought to Soma by {{jalink|Sontoku Ninomiya|Q968964}} and the Ninomiya family. {{jalink|Mannobu|Q1193862}} In the spring of the first year of the Koshin year ({{jalink|1860|Q7691}}), the restoration of the shrine shrine of Kanmine Shrine was ordered, and master craftsman Uesugi Shudono, a master craftsman from Aizu, constructed the shrine. In addition to the two Kanmine Shrines in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, and Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Keisoku Shrine, located in Kitaebi, Kashima Ward, also called itself Kanmine Shrine, but Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward It has been called Kanmine Shrine since ancient times, and the {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} is said to have a history of more than a thousand years, so our company was considered to be the headquarters of Kanmine Shrine. File:Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine at Kashima ward,Minami-soma city.JPG|Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine (Kitaebi, Kashima-ku, Minami-soma city) === Precincts === * The shrine building is located on the hill on the west side of {{jalink|Tokiwa Expressway|Q1059875}}{{jalink|Minamisoma Interchange|Q10908775}}. * At the entrance to the shrine is the first torii gate with a plaque reading ``Kanreisha.'' From there, climb up the stone steps for about 200 meters to reach the second torii gate of Kanmine Shrine. File:View of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Distant view of Kanmine-jinja shrine enshrined place File:1st torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine First Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach entrance File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Sando File:2nd torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine second torii gate File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Sando File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Front part of the shrine File:Carving of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Carving of the worship hall
There are carvings of rainbow beams, toaded dragons, phoenixes, plants, wood-nosed lions, etc. File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine main hall === Source === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Oku Soshi (Soma City History 4) Soma City 1969 * Engishiki Kanmine Shrine abbreviation All parishioners April 1994 * Kanmine Shrine, one of the eight shrines Haramachi City Board of Education {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakamine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{'text': '福島県南相馬市原町区信田沢', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakamine-jinja}} [[JA:冠嶺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11395049}} == Japanese content == '''Kanmine Shrine'' (Sakamine Shrine) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisoma City|Q642094}}. {{jalink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Engishiki}}{{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} It is one of eight troupes located in {{jalink|Namegata District (Fukushima Prefecture)|Q11626139|Namegata District}}. Two shrines located in Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, and Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward are said to be the main nonsha. === Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|270px]]
Haden of worship |Location={{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisoma City|Q642094}}{{jalink|Kashima Ward|Q24895757}}Kamitochikubo Miyashita 35 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward)}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukunahikona no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsuhikohikonionigeiminoto}}
{{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}}
(den) {{jalink|Yamikamikami|Q644201|Yamitakamikami}} |Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} |Founded={{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Also known as Hachiryu Gongen/Hachiryu Daimyojin |Regular Festival={{jalink|April 8th|Q2503}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * According to the history book in the precincts, {{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are enshrined here. ** According to the Shikinai-sha Kensho-kai, the current enshrined deity is {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsuhiko Hibunigei no Mikoto}} Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto. ** According to ``Okusōshi'', it is said to enshrine the three shrines of Amatsuhiko Hinaunigei no Mikoto, Amatsuhiko Hikoidemi no Mikoto, and {{jalink|Yokamijin|Q644201|Hachiryu Daimyojin}}. * Both the {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532|Goshintai}} of {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsuhiko Hidemige no Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are said to be "statues of horsemen." === History === According to historical records, during the era of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}}, {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Nippon Takeru}} came to Oshu after receiving an imperial order to conquer Toi. At that time, they received a spiritual message from {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}, the landowner's god, and they solicited the Emperor's grandson (Amatsuhiko-Kainonige-no-Mikoto) and performed rituals for 17 days. The mountain where he camped was surrounded by rivers on all sides, and it is said that Nippon Takeru was able to take advantage of the geographical advantage and defeat the bandits and successfully pacify the area. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto, a landowner deity, was also known as Dosojin and Dorikujin. It is said that the mountain where Kanmine Shrine was built was named ``Dorokujinzan'' (currently Dorokujinzan) because it was the mountain where the god Dorikami had miraculous experiences. At the southern foot of Mt. Dorikujinzan, a waterhole called ``Mano Pond'' was established with water from Amano Manai and was used as a wash basin for Kanminejin. The name Mano River is said to have come from this ``Mano Pond.'' Additionally, there is a strange rock called ``Tateishi'' upstream of the Mano River, which is said to be the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto descended and was enshrined.During the {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193549}} era, which was the era of {{jalink|Emperor Reizei|Q374125}}, a ceremony was held to pray for peace in the land by holding a {{jalink|Hotori|Q11675302}}. However, during the subsequent 13 years leading up to the {{jalink|Kohei|Q1193572}} year, wars such as {{jalink|Zenunen no Eki|Q1187448}} occurred, and the shrine grounds were confiscated and fell into disrepair, with only a small shrine erected. During the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Gohorikawa|Q357221}}, a shrine was built in the village at the request of parishioners, and the object of worship was moved from Dorikushinzan to its current enshrined place in {{jalink|Hiroki|Q1194200}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1230|Q5412}}) {{jalink|April 8th|Q2503}}. After that, at the request of the parishioners, {{jalink|Kenno Shrine|Q11616108}} ({{jalink|Watatsumi Shrine in Iitate Village, which is a branch shrine of Kakeno Shrine in Namie Town) (Iitate Village||Watatsumi Shrine}}), {{jalink|Yamyojin|Q644201|Yamiyoshikami}}, called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'', was enshrined together as a side shrine. For this reason, the company name was changed to Hachiryu Shrine, and it came to be called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'' by the people of the county. Since then, it has been dedicated to three deities, and rituals such as riding down the beach have also started to be held. According to the description, a mikoshi is carried out for the Hamakudari ritual on {{jalink|July 7|Q2694}}, and is enshrined at Kumaki Akira Shrine in the Kadokawara area at night. It is said that in the morning of the next morning, people descended on the beach in the present-day Minami-ebi area to pray for peace and peace in the world, long life for the military, abundant harvests, and safety for all people.Sukuhikona no Mikoto, one of the current deities enshrined, was discovered according to a Shikinai shrine survey in the county in {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}). Hachiryu Shrine is thought to be the Ronsha of Kanmine Shrine, and after the name was changed to Kanmine Shrine in 1825 (Bunsei 8), it is thought to have been enshrined together with Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward. === Precincts === * Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, main hall) * In addition, there are stone shrines and precinct shrines located within the precincts. * The {{jalink|Chinju-no-Mori|Q11651158}} within the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Minamisoma City as the ``Kanmine Shrine Forest''{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Kashima's Cultural Properties|url=http://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunnkazai/b-kashima/kabun.jsp|work= |publisher=Minamisoma City|date= |accessdate=2012-11-09}}. Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} | Founded = unknown |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Regular Festival={{jalink|April 17th|Q2520}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Sukunahikona||Sukahikona no Mikoto}} is enshrined here. === History === Its founding is unknown, but it is said to have declined with the passage of time, and only a small shrine remained. Furthermore, the shrine building was destroyed by a wild fire in ancient times, and after that a shrine was built to enshrine it. {{jalink|Hoei|Q1145262}} from {{jalink|Mashinori (Japan)|Q1193848|Mashinori}} ({{jalink|1704|Q6824}}-{{jalink|1715|Q6884}}) When the family of Kudayu Ota, who was a shrine official, died out, Sankaku-in, a Haguro-ha Shugen, performed the rituals in their place. It is said that the number of residents decreased from the {{jalink|Tenmei|Q1194121}} era, and by the Bunka era, both the village and the shrine were in ruins. It is said that a local farmer moved the object of worship to a small shrine in a private house, hid it, and for many years it was enshrined by a graduate school of Honzan-ha Shugen Shugen. As time passed and these farmers disappeared, the fact that Kanmine no Kami was once enshrined in a small shrine in the village was forgotten.A man called the Sato clan came from another village and began living in the mansion of a farmer who was worshiping the Kanmine deity. While Mr. Sato was engaged in farming, he repaired and worshiped a small shrine on the mountain of Sakamine. {{jalink|Culture (Era name)|Q1373146|Culture}}12 ({{jalink|1815|Q6948}}) Year of the Pig, {{jalink|Soma Nakamura Domain|Q844079|Ao {{jalink|Masutane Souma|Q8514605}}, the lord of the horse clan, ordered the shrine priest and a person named Mitsuna Watabe to investigate and identify eight Shikinai shrines in Namegata district. As a result, {{jalink|Taka Shrine|Q11430895}}, {{jalink|Kashima Miko Shrine|Q11677124}}, {{jalink|Mito Shrine|Q11488802}}, {{jalink|Oshio Shrine}}, {{jalink|Koza Shrine|Q11669904}} and {{jalink|Hisatsu Shrine}} were identified, but Kanmine Shrine and {{jalink|Masutamine Shrine}} could not be identified because it was unclear which shrines they were referring to. {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}) In the year of the Rabbit, another investigation order is given. The county official heard that there was a small shrine dedicated to the god of Kanmine in the village and investigated, but the villagers, who became suspicious, denied the existence of Kanmine Shrine, and as a result, Kanmine Shrine was even more forgotten from history.In the {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}} years, thanks to {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}, the financial reconstruction of the Soma clan and the reconstruction of devastated farming villages progressed thanks to the ``Kokoku Anminho'', an agricultural reconstruction measure brought to Soma by {{jalink|Sontoku Ninomiya|Q968964}} and the Ninomiya family. {{jalink|Mannobu|Q1193862}} In the spring of the first year of the Koshin year ({{jalink|1860|Q7691}}), the restoration of the shrine shrine of Kanmine Shrine was ordered, and master craftsman Uesugi Shudono, a master craftsman from Aizu, constructed the shrine. In addition to the two Kanmine Shrines in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, and Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Keisoku Shrine, located in Kitaebi, Kashima Ward, also called itself Kanmine Shrine, but Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward It has been called Kanmine Shrine since ancient times, and the {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} is said to have a history of more than a thousand years, so our company was considered to be the headquarters of Kanmine Shrine. File:Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine at Kashima ward,Minami-soma city.JPG|Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine (Kitaebi, Kashima-ku, Minami-soma city) === Precincts === * The shrine building is located on the hill on the west side of {{jalink|Tokiwa Expressway|Q1059875}}{{jalink|Minamisoma Interchange|Q10908775}}. * At the entrance to the shrine is the first torii gate with a plaque reading ``Kanreisha.'' From there, climb up the stone steps for about 200 meters to reach the second torii gate of Kanmine Shrine. File:View of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Distant view of Kanmine-jinja shrine enshrined place File:1st torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine First Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach entrance File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Sando File:2nd torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine second torii gate File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Sando File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Front part of the shrine File:Carving of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Carving of the worship hall
There are carvings of rainbow beams, toaded dragons, phoenixes, plants, wood-nosed lions, etc. File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine main hall === Source === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Oku Soshi (Soma City History 4) Soma City 1969 * Engishiki Kanmine Shrine abbreviation All parishioners April 1994 * Kanmine Shrine, one of the eight shrines Haramachi City Board of Education {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakamine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]
