Shrine|WD=Q135040018}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県南相馬市鹿島区上栃窪宮下35', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '福島県南相馬市原町区信田沢', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakamine-jinja}} [[JA:冠嶺神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11395049}} == Japanese content == '''Kanmine Shrine'' (Sakamine Shrine) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma City|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}. {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} It is one of eight troupes located in {{ill|lt=Namegata District (Fukushima Prefecture)|Q11626139|WD=Q11626139|lt=Namegata District}}. Two shrines located in Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, and Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward are said to be the main nonsha. === Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|270px]]
Haden of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma City|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}{{ill|lt=Kashima Ward|Q24895757|WD=Q24895757}}Kamitochikubo Miyashita 35 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhikohikonionigeiminoto}}
{{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}}
(den) {{ill|lt=Yamikamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Yamitakamikami}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Also known as Hachiryu Gongen/Hachiryu Daimyojin |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April 8th|Q2503|WD=Q2503}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * According to the history book in the precincts, {{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are enshrined here. ** According to the Shikinai-sha Kensho-kai, the current enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhiko Hibunigei no Mikoto}} Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto. ** According to ``Okusōshi'', it is said to enshrine the three shrines of Amatsuhiko Hinaunigei no Mikoto, Amatsuhiko Hikoidemi no Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Yokamijin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Hachiryu Daimyojin}}. * Both the {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|lt=Goshintai}} of {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemige no Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are said to be "statues of horsemen." === History === According to historical records, during the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nippon Takeru}} came to Oshu after receiving an imperial order to conquer Toi. At that time, they received a spiritual message from {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}, the landowner's god, and they solicited the Emperor's grandson (Amatsuhiko-Kainonige-no-Mikoto) and performed rituals for 17 days. The mountain where he camped was surrounded by rivers on all sides, and it is said that Nippon Takeru was able to take advantage of the geographical advantage and defeat the bandits and successfully pacify the area. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto, a landowner deity, was also known as Dosojin and Dorikujin. It is said that the mountain where Kanmine Shrine was built was named ``Dorokujinzan'' (currently Dorokujinzan) because it was the mountain where the god Dorikami had miraculous experiences. At the southern foot of Mt. Dorikujinzan, a waterhole called ``Mano Pond'' was established with water from Amano Manai and was used as a wash basin for Kanminejin. The name Mano River is said to have come from this ``Mano Pond.'' Additionally, there is a strange rock called ``Tateishi'' upstream of the Mano River, which is said to be the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto descended and was enshrined.During the {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193549|WD=Q1193549}} era, which was the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Reizei|Q374125|WD=Q374125}}, a ceremony was held to pray for peace in the land by holding a {{ill|lt=Hotori|Q11675302|WD=Q11675302}}. However, during the subsequent 13 years leading up to the {{ill|lt=Kohei|Q1193572|WD=Q1193572}} year, wars such as {{ill|lt=Zenunen no Eki|Q1187448|WD=Q1187448}} occurred, and the shrine grounds were confiscated and fell into disrepair, with only a small shrine erected. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Gohorikawa|Q357221|WD=Q357221}}, a shrine was built in the village at the request of parishioners, and the object of worship was moved from Dorikushinzan to its current enshrined place in {{ill|lt=Hiroki|Q1194200|WD=Q1194200}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1230|Q5412|WD=Q5412}}) {{ill|lt=April 8th|Q2503|WD=Q2503}}. After that, at the request of the parishioners, {{ill|lt=Kenno Shrine|Q11616108|WD=Q11616108}} ({{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine in Iitate Village, which is a branch shrine of Kakeno Shrine in Namie Town) (Iitate Village|UNKNOWN|lt=Watatsumi Shrine}}), {{ill|lt=Yamyojin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Yamiyoshikami}}, called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'', was enshrined together as a side shrine. For this reason, the company name was changed to Hachiryu Shrine, and it came to be called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'' by the people of the county. Since then, it has been dedicated to three deities, and rituals such as riding down the beach have also started to be held. According to the description, a mikoshi is carried out for the Hamakudari ritual on {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694}}, and is enshrined at Kumaki Akira Shrine in the Kadokawara area at night. It is said that in the morning of the next morning, people descended on the beach in the present-day Minami-ebi area to pray for peace and peace in the world, long life for the military, abundant harvests, and safety for all people.Sukuhikona no Mikoto, one of the current deities enshrined, was discovered according to a Shikinai shrine survey in the county in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}}2 ({{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967}}). Hachiryu Shrine is thought to be the Ronsha of Kanmine Shrine, and after the name was changed to Kanmine Shrine in 1825 (Bunsei 8), it is thought to have been enshrined together with Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward. === Precincts === * Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, main hall) * In addition, there are stone shrines and precinct shrines located within the precincts. * The {{ill|lt=Chinju-no-Mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158}} within the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Minamisoma City as the ``Kanmine Shrine Forest''{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Kashima's Cultural Properties|url=http://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunnkazai/b-kashima/kabun.jsp|work= |publisher=Minamisoma City|date= |accessdate=2012-11-09}}. File:Torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine File:Small shrines of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts {{-}} === Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma City|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}}194 Shinoda Sawatonouchi |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | Founded = unknown |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |alias= |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April 17th|Q2520|WD=Q2520}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukahikona no Mikoto}} is enshrined here. === History === Its founding is unknown, but it is said to have declined with the passage of time, and only a small shrine remained. Furthermore, the shrine building was destroyed by a wild fire in ancient times, and after that a shrine was built to enshrine it. {{ill|lt=Hoei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} from {{ill|lt=Mashinori (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Mashinori}} ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}-{{ill|lt=1715|Q6884|WD=Q6884}}) When the family of Kudayu Ota, who was a shrine official, died out, Sankaku-in, a Haguro-ha Shugen, performed the rituals in their place. It is said that the number of residents decreased from the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121}} era, and by the Bunka era, both the village and the shrine were in ruins. It is said that a local farmer moved the object of worship to a small shrine in a private house, hid it, and for many years it was enshrined by a graduate school of Honzan-ha Shugen Shugen. As time passed and these farmers disappeared, the fact that Kanmine no Kami was once enshrined in a small shrine in the village was forgotten.A man called the Sato clan came from another village and began living in the mansion of a farmer who was worshiping the Kanmine deity. While Mr. Sato was engaged in farming, he repaired and worshiped a small shrine on the mountain of Sakamine. {{ill|lt=Culture (Era name)|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Culture}}12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|WD=Q6948}}) Year of the Pig, {{jalink|Soma Nakamura Domain|Q844079|Ao {{ill|lt=Masutane Souma|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605}}, the lord of the horse clan, ordered the shrine priest and a person named Mitsuna Watabe to investigate and identify eight Shikinai shrines in Namegata district. As a result, {{ill|lt=Taka Shrine|Q11430895|WD=Q11430895}}, {{ill|lt=Kashima Miko Shrine|Q11677124|WD=Q11677124}}, {{ill|lt=Mito Shrine|Q11488802|WD=Q11488802}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshio Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Koza Shrine|Q11669904|WD=Q11669904}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hisatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} were identified, but Kanmine Shrine and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masutamine Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} could not be identified because it was unclear which shrines they were referring to. {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}}2 ({{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967}}) In the year of the Rabbit, another investigation order is given. The county official heard that there was a small shrine dedicated to the god of Kanmine in the village and investigated, but the villagers, who became suspicious, denied the existence of Kanmine Shrine, and as a result, Kanmine Shrine was even more forgotten from history.In the {{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|WD=Q1327259}} years, thanks to {{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|WD=Q1327259}}, the financial reconstruction of the Soma clan and the reconstruction of devastated farming villages progressed thanks to the ``Kokoku Anminho'', an agricultural reconstruction measure brought to Soma by {{ill|lt=Sontoku Ninomiya|Q968964|WD=Q968964}} and the Ninomiya family. {{ill|lt=Mannobu|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} In the spring of the first year of the Koshin year ({{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}}), the restoration of the shrine shrine of Kanmine Shrine was ordered, and master craftsman Uesugi Shudono, a master craftsman from Aizu, constructed the shrine. In addition to the two Kanmine Shrines in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, and Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Keisoku Shrine, located in Kitaebi, Kashima Ward, also called itself Kanmine Shrine, but Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward It has been called Kanmine Shrine since ancient times, and the {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is said to have a history of more than a thousand years, so our company was considered to be the headquarters of Kanmine Shrine. File:Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine at Kashima ward,Minami-soma city.JPG|Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine (Kitaebi, Kashima-ku, Minami-soma city) === Precincts === * The shrine building is located on the hill on the west side of {{ill|lt=Tokiwa Expressway|Q1059875|WD=Q1059875}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma Interchange|Q10908775|WD=Q10908775}}. * At the entrance to the shrine is the first torii gate with a plaque reading ``Kanreisha.'' From there, climb up the stone steps for about 200 meters to reach the second torii gate of Kanmine Shrine. File:View of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Distant view of Kanmine-jinja shrine enshrined place File:1st torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine First Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach entrance File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Sando File:2nd torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine second torii gate File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Sando File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Front part of the shrine File:Carving of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Carving of the worship hall
There are carvings of rainbow beams, toaded dragons, phoenixes, plants, wood-nosed lions, etc. File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine main hall === Source === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Oku Soshi (Soma City History 4) Soma City 1969 * Engishiki Kanmine Shrine abbreviation All parishioners April 1994 * Kanmine Shrine, one of the eight shrines Haramachi City Board of Education {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakamine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

(den) {{ill|lt=Yamikamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Yamitakamikami}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Also known as Hachiryu Gongen/Hachiryu Daimyojin |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April 8th|Q2503|WD=Q2503}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * According to the history book in the precincts, {{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are enshrined here. ** According to the Shikinai-sha Kensho-kai, the current enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhiko Hibunigei no Mikoto}} Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto. ** According to ``Okusōshi'', it is said to enshrine the three shrines of Amatsuhiko Hinaunigei no Mikoto, Amatsuhiko Hikoidemi no Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Yokamijin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Hachiryu Daimyojin}}. * Both the {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|lt=Goshintai}} of {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemige no Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are said to be "statues of horsemen." === History === According to historical records, during the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nippon Takeru}} came to Oshu after receiving an imperial order to conquer Toi. At that time, they received a spiritual message from {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}, the landowner's god, and they solicited the Emperor's grandson (Amatsuhiko-Kainonige-no-Mikoto) and performed rituals for 17 days. The mountain where he camped was surrounded by rivers on all sides, and it is said that Nippon Takeru was able to take advantage of the geographical advantage and defeat the bandits and successfully pacify the area. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto, a landowner deity, was also known as Dosojin and Dorikujin. It is said that the mountain where Kanmine Shrine was built was named ``Dorokujinzan'' (currently Dorokujinzan) because it was the mountain where the god Dorikami had miraculous experiences. At the southern foot of Mt. Dorikujinzan, a waterhole called ``Mano Pond'' was established with water from Amano Manai and was used as a wash basin for Kanminejin. The name Mano River is said to have come from this ``Mano Pond.'' Additionally, there is a strange rock called ``Tateishi'' upstream of the Mano River, which is said to be the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto descended and was enshrined.During the {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193549|WD=Q1193549}} era, which was the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Reizei|Q374125|WD=Q374125}}, a ceremony was held to pray for peace in the land by holding a {{ill|lt=Hotori|Q11675302|WD=Q11675302}}. However, during the subsequent 13 years leading up to the {{ill|lt=Kohei|Q1193572|WD=Q1193572}} year, wars such as {{ill|lt=Zenunen no Eki|Q1187448|WD=Q1187448}} occurred, and the shrine grounds were confiscated and fell into disrepair, with only a small shrine erected. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Gohorikawa|Q357221|WD=Q357221}}, a shrine was built in the village at the request of parishioners, and the object of worship was moved from Dorikushinzan to its current enshrined place in {{ill|lt=Hiroki|Q1194200|WD=Q1194200}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1230|Q5412|WD=Q5412}}) {{ill|lt=April 8th|Q2503|WD=Q2503}}. After that, at the request of the parishioners, {{ill|lt=Kenno Shrine|Q11616108|WD=Q11616108}} ({{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine in Iitate Village, which is a branch shrine of Kakeno Shrine in Namie Town) (Iitate Village|UNKNOWN|lt=Watatsumi Shrine}}), {{ill|lt=Yamyojin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Yamiyoshikami}}, called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'', was enshrined together as a side shrine. For this reason, the company name was changed to Hachiryu Shrine, and it came to be called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'' by the people of the county. Since then, it has been dedicated to three deities, and rituals such as riding down the beach have also started to be held. According to the description, a mikoshi is carried out for the Hamakudari ritual on {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694}}, and is enshrined at Kumaki Akira Shrine in the Kadokawara area at night. It is said that in the morning of the next morning, people descended on the beach in the present-day Minami-ebi area to pray for peace and peace in the world, long life for the military, abundant harvests, and safety for all people.Sukuhikona no Mikoto, one of the current deities enshrined, was discovered according to a Shikinai shrine survey in the county in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}}2 ({{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967}}). Hachiryu Shrine is thought to be the Ronsha of Kanmine Shrine, and after the name was changed to Kanmine Shrine in 1825 (Bunsei 8), it is thought to have been enshrined together with Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward. === Precincts === * Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, main hall) * In addition, there are stone shrines and precinct shrines located within the precincts. * The {{ill|lt=Chinju-no-Mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158}} within the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Minamisoma City as the ``Kanmine Shrine Forest''{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Kashima's Cultural Properties|url=http://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunnkazai/b-kashima/kabun.jsp|work= |publisher=Minamisoma City|date= |accessdate=2012-11-09}}. File:Torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine File:Small shrines of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts {{-}} === Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma City|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}}194 Shinoda Sawatonouchi |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | Founded = unknown |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |alias= |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April 17th|Q2520|WD=Q2520}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukahikona no Mikoto}} is enshrined here. === History === Its founding is unknown, but it is said to have declined with the passage of time, and only a small shrine remained. Furthermore, the shrine building was destroyed by a wild fire in ancient times, and after that a shrine was built to enshrine it. {{ill|lt=Hoei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} from {{ill|lt=Mashinori (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Mashinori}} ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}-{{ill|lt=1715|Q6884|WD=Q6884}}) When the family of Kudayu Ota, who was a shrine official, died out, Sankaku-in, a Haguro-ha Shugen, performed the rituals in their place. It is said that the number of residents decreased from the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121}} era, and by the Bunka era, both the village and the shrine were in ruins. It is said that a local farmer moved the object of worship to a small shrine in a private house, hid it, and for many years it was enshrined by a graduate school of Honzan-ha Shugen Shugen. As time passed and these farmers disappeared, the fact that Kanmine no Kami was once enshrined in a small shrine in the village was forgotten.A man called the Sato clan came from another village and began living in the mansion of a farmer who was worshiping the Kanmine deity. While Mr. Sato was engaged in farming, he repaired and worshiped a small shrine on the mountain of Sakamine. {{ill|lt=Culture (Era name)|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Culture}}12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|WD=Q6948}}) Year of the Pig, {{jalink|Soma Nakamura Domain|Q844079|Ao {{ill|lt=Masutane Souma|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605}}, the lord of the horse clan, ordered the shrine priest and a person named Mitsuna Watabe to investigate and identify eight Shikinai shrines in Namegata district. As a result, {{ill|lt=Taka Shrine|Q11430895|WD=Q11430895}}, {{ill|lt=Kashima Miko Shrine|Q11677124|WD=Q11677124}}, {{ill|lt=Mito Shrine|Q11488802|WD=Q11488802}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshio Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Koza Shrine|Q11669904|WD=Q11669904}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hisatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} were identified, but Kanmine Shrine and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masutamine Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} could not be identified because it was unclear which shrines they were referring to. {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}}2 ({{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967}}) In the year of the Rabbit, another investigation order is given. The county official heard that there was a small shrine dedicated to the god of Kanmine in the village and investigated, but the villagers, who became suspicious, denied the existence of Kanmine Shrine, and as a result, Kanmine Shrine was even more forgotten from history.In the {{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|WD=Q1327259}} years, thanks to {{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|WD=Q1327259}}, the financial reconstruction of the Soma clan and the reconstruction of devastated farming villages progressed thanks to the ``Kokoku Anminho'', an agricultural reconstruction measure brought to Soma by {{ill|lt=Sontoku Ninomiya|Q968964|WD=Q968964}} and the Ninomiya family. {{ill|lt=Mannobu|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} In the spring of the first year of the Koshin year ({{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}}), the restoration of the shrine shrine of Kanmine Shrine was ordered, and master craftsman Uesugi Shudono, a master craftsman from Aizu, constructed the shrine. In addition to the two Kanmine Shrines in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, and Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Keisoku Shrine, located in Kitaebi, Kashima Ward, also called itself Kanmine Shrine, but Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward It has been called Kanmine Shrine since ancient times, and the {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is said to have a history of more than a thousand years, so our company was considered to be the headquarters of Kanmine Shrine. File:Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine at Kashima ward,Minami-soma city.JPG|Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine (Kitaebi, Kashima-ku, Minami-soma city) === Precincts === * The shrine building is located on the hill on the west side of {{ill|lt=Tokiwa Expressway|Q1059875|WD=Q1059875}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma Interchange|Q10908775|WD=Q10908775}}. * At the entrance to the shrine is the first torii gate with a plaque reading ``Kanreisha.'' From there, climb up the stone steps for about 200 meters to reach the second torii gate of Kanmine Shrine. File:View of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Distant view of Kanmine-jinja shrine enshrined place File:1st torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine First Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach entrance File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Sando File:2nd torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine second torii gate File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Sando File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Front part of the shrine File:Carving of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Carving of the worship hall
There are carvings of rainbow beams, toaded dragons, phoenixes, plants, wood-nosed lions, etc. File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine main hall === Source === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Oku Soshi (Soma City History 4) Soma City 1969 * Engishiki Kanmine Shrine abbreviation All parishioners April 1994 * Kanmine Shrine, one of the eight shrines Haramachi City Board of Education {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakamine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma City|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}. {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} It is one of eight troupes located in {{ill|lt=Namegata District (Fukushima Prefecture)|Q11626139|WD=Q11626139|lt=Namegata District}}. Two shrines located in Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, and Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward are said to be the main nonsha. === Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|270px]]
Haden of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma City|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}{{ill|lt=Kashima Ward|Q24895757|WD=Q24895757}}Kamitochikubo Miyashita 35 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto}}

Old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Also known as Hachiryu Gongen/Hachiryu Daimyojin |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April 8th|Q2503|WD=Q2503}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * According to the history book in the precincts, {{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are enshrined here. ** According to the Shikinai-sha Kensho-kai, the current enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhiko Hibunigei no Mikoto}} Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto. ** According to ``Okusōshi'', it is said to enshrine the three shrines of Amatsuhiko Hinaunigei no Mikoto, Amatsuhiko Hikoidemi no Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Yokamijin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Hachiryu Daimyojin}}. * Both the {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|lt=Goshintai}} of {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemige no Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto}} are said to be "statues of horsemen." === History === According to historical records, during the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nippon Takeru}} came to Oshu after receiving an imperial order to conquer Toi. At that time, they received a spiritual message from {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}, the landowner's god, and they solicited the Emperor's grandson (Amatsuhiko-Kainonige-no-Mikoto) and performed rituals for 17 days. The mountain where he camped was surrounded by rivers on all sides, and it is said that Nippon Takeru was able to take advantage of the geographical advantage and defeat the bandits and successfully pacify the area. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto, a landowner deity, was also known as Dosojin and Dorikujin. It is said that the mountain where Kanmine Shrine was built was named ``Dorokujinzan'' (currently Dorokujinzan) because it was the mountain where the god Dorikami had miraculous experiences. At the southern foot of Mt. Dorikujinzan, a waterhole called ``Mano Pond'' was established with water from Amano Manai and was used as a wash basin for Kanminejin. The name Mano River is said to have come from this ``Mano Pond.'' Additionally, there is a strange rock called ``Tateishi'' upstream of the Mano River, which is said to be the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto descended and was enshrined.During the {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193549|WD=Q1193549}} era, which was the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Reizei|Q374125|WD=Q374125}}, a ceremony was held to pray for peace in the land by holding a {{ill|lt=Hotori|Q11675302|WD=Q11675302}}. However, during the subsequent 13 years leading up to the {{ill|lt=Kohei|Q1193572|WD=Q1193572}} year, wars such as {{ill|lt=Zenunen no Eki|Q1187448|WD=Q1187448}} occurred, and the shrine grounds were confiscated and fell into disrepair, with only a small shrine erected. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Gohorikawa|Q357221|WD=Q357221}}, a shrine was built in the village at the request of parishioners, and the object of worship was moved from Dorikushinzan to its current enshrined place in {{ill|lt=Hiroki|Q1194200|WD=Q1194200}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1230|Q5412|WD=Q5412}}) {{ill|lt=April 8th|Q2503|WD=Q2503}}. After that, at the request of the parishioners, {{ill|lt=Kenno Shrine|Q11616108|WD=Q11616108}} ({{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine in Iitate Village, which is a branch shrine of Kakeno Shrine in Namie Town) (Iitate Village|UNKNOWN|lt=Watatsumi Shrine}}), {{ill|lt=Yamyojin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Yamiyoshikami}}, called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'', was enshrined together as a side shrine. For this reason, the company name was changed to Hachiryu Shrine, and it came to be called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'' by the people of the county. Since then, it has been dedicated to three deities, and rituals such as riding down the beach have also started to be held. According to the description, a mikoshi is carried out for the Hamakudari ritual on {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694}}, and is enshrined at Kumaki Akira Shrine in the Kadokawara area at night. It is said that in the morning of the next morning, people descended on the beach in the present-day Minami-ebi area to pray for peace and peace in the world, long life for the military, abundant harvests, and safety for all people.Sukuhikona no Mikoto, one of the current deities enshrined, was discovered according to a Shikinai shrine survey in the county in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}}2 ({{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967}}). Hachiryu Shrine is thought to be the Ronsha of Kanmine Shrine, and after the name was changed to Kanmine Shrine in 1825 (Bunsei 8), it is thought to have been enshrined together with Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward. === Precincts === * Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, main hall) * In addition, there are stone shrines and precinct shrines located within the precincts. * The {{ill|lt=Chinju-no-Mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158}} within the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Minamisoma City as the ``Kanmine Shrine Forest''{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Kashima's Cultural Properties|url=http://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunnkazai/b-kashima/kabun.jsp|work= |publisher=Minamisoma City|date= |accessdate=2012-11-09}}. File:Torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine File:Small shrines of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts {{-}} === Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma City|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}}194 Shinoda Sawatonouchi |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | Founded = unknown |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |alias= |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April 17th|Q2520|WD=Q2520}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukahikona no Mikoto}} is enshrined here. === History === Its founding is unknown, but it is said to have declined with the passage of time, and only a small shrine remained. Furthermore, the shrine building was destroyed by a wild fire in ancient times, and after that a shrine was built to enshrine it. {{ill|lt=Hoei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} from {{ill|lt=Mashinori (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Mashinori}} ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}-{{ill|lt=1715|Q6884|WD=Q6884}}) When the family of Kudayu Ota, who was a shrine official, died out, Sankaku-in, a Haguro-ha Shugen, performed the rituals in their place. It is said that the number of residents decreased from the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121}} era, and by the Bunka era, both the village and the shrine were in ruins. It is said that a local farmer moved the object of worship to a small shrine in a private house, hid it, and for many years it was enshrined by a graduate school of Honzan-ha Shugen Shugen. As time passed and these farmers disappeared, the fact that Kanmine no Kami was once enshrined in a small shrine in the village was forgotten.A man called the Sato clan came from another village and began living in the mansion of a farmer who was worshiping the Kanmine deity. While Mr. Sato was engaged in farming, he repaired and worshiped a small shrine on the mountain of Sakamine. {{ill|lt=Culture (Era name)|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Culture}}12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|WD=Q6948}}) Year of the Pig, {{jalink|Soma Nakamura Domain|Q844079|Ao {{ill|lt=Masutane Souma|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605}}, the lord of the horse clan, ordered the shrine priest and a person named Mitsuna Watabe to investigate and identify eight Shikinai shrines in Namegata district. As a result, {{ill|lt=Taka Shrine|Q11430895|WD=Q11430895}}, {{ill|lt=Kashima Miko Shrine|Q11677124|WD=Q11677124}}, {{ill|lt=Mito Shrine|Q11488802|WD=Q11488802}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshio Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Koza Shrine|Q11669904|WD=Q11669904}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hisatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} were identified, but Kanmine Shrine and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masutamine Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} could not be identified because it was unclear which shrines they were referring to. {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}}2 ({{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967}}) In the year of the Rabbit, another investigation order is given. The county official heard that there was a small shrine dedicated to the god of Kanmine in the village and investigated, but the villagers, who became suspicious, denied the existence of Kanmine Shrine, and as a result, Kanmine Shrine was even more forgotten from history.In the {{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|WD=Q1327259}} years, thanks to {{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|WD=Q1327259}}, the financial reconstruction of the Soma clan and the reconstruction of devastated farming villages progressed thanks to the ``Kokoku Anminho'', an agricultural reconstruction measure brought to Soma by {{ill|lt=Sontoku Ninomiya|Q968964|WD=Q968964}} and the Ninomiya family. {{ill|lt=Mannobu|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} In the spring of the first year of the Koshin year ({{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}}), the restoration of the shrine shrine of Kanmine Shrine was ordered, and master craftsman Uesugi Shudono, a master craftsman from Aizu, constructed the shrine. In addition to the two Kanmine Shrines in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, and Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Keisoku Shrine, located in Kitaebi, Kashima Ward, also called itself Kanmine Shrine, but Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward It has been called Kanmine Shrine since ancient times, and the {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is said to have a history of more than a thousand years, so our company was considered to be the headquarters of Kanmine Shrine. File:Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine at Kashima ward,Minami-soma city.JPG|Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine (Kitaebi, Kashima-ku, Minami-soma city) === Precincts === * The shrine building is located on the hill on the west side of {{ill|lt=Tokiwa Expressway|Q1059875|WD=Q1059875}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma Interchange|Q10908775|WD=Q10908775}}. * At the entrance to the shrine is the first torii gate with a plaque reading ``Kanreisha.'' From there, climb up the stone steps for about 200 meters to reach the second torii gate of Kanmine Shrine. File:View of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Distant view of Kanmine-jinja shrine enshrined place File:1st torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine First Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach entrance File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Sando File:2nd torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine second torii gate File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Sando File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Front part of the shrine File:Carving of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Carving of the worship hall
There are carvings of rainbow beams, toaded dragons, phoenixes, plants, wood-nosed lions, etc. File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine main hall === Source === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Oku Soshi (Soma City History 4) Soma City 1969 * Engishiki Kanmine Shrine abbreviation All parishioners April 1994 * Kanmine Shrine, one of the eight shrines Haramachi City Board of Education {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakamine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma City|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}. {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}} It is one of eight troupes located in {{ill|lt=Namegata District (Fukushima Prefecture)|Q11626139|WD=Q11626139|lt=Namegata District|1=Namekata district}}. Two shrines located in Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, and Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward are said to be the main nonsha. === Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|270px]]
Haden of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma City|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}{{ill|lt=Kashima Ward|Q24895757|WD=Q24895757|1=Kashima-ku}}Kamitochikubo Miyashita 35 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto}}

{{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto|1=Hoori}}
(den) {{ill|lt=Yamikamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Yamitakamikami|1=Kuraokami}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Also known as Hachiryu Gongen/Hachiryu Daimyojin |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April 8th|Q2503|WD=Q2503|1=April 8}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * According to the history book in the precincts, {{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto|1=Hoori}} are enshrined here. ** According to the Shikinai-sha Kensho-kai, the current enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhiko Hibunigei no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto. ** According to ``Okusōshi'', it is said to enshrine the three shrines of Amatsuhiko Hinaunigei no Mikoto, Amatsuhiko Hikoidemi no Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Yokamijin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Hachiryu Daimyojin|1=Kuraokami}}. * Both the {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|lt=Goshintai|1=Shintai}} of {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemige no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto|1=Hoori}} are said to be "statues of horsemen." === History === According to historical records, during the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nippon Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} came to Oshu after receiving an imperial order to conquer Toi. At that time, they received a spiritual message from {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}, the landowner's god, and they solicited the Emperor's grandson (Amatsuhiko-Kainonige-no-Mikoto) and performed rituals for 17 days. The mountain where he camped was surrounded by rivers on all sides, and it is said that Nippon Takeru was able to take advantage of the geographical advantage and defeat the bandits and successfully pacify the area. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto, a landowner deity, was also known as Dosojin and Dorikujin. It is said that the mountain where Kanmine Shrine was built was named ``Dorokujinzan'' (currently Dorokujinzan) because it was the mountain where the god Dorikami had miraculous experiences. At the southern foot of Mt. Dorikujinzan, a waterhole called ``Mano Pond'' was established with water from Amano Manai and was used as a wash basin for Kanminejin. The name Mano River is said to have come from this ``Mano Pond.'' Additionally, there is a strange rock called ``Tateishi'' upstream of the Mano River, which is said to be the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto descended and was enshrined.During the {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193549|WD=Q1193549|1=Eishō (Heian period)}} era, which was the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Reizei|Q374125|WD=Q374125|1=Emperor Reizei}}, a ceremony was held to pray for peace in the land by holding a {{ill|lt=Hotori|Q11675302|WD=Q11675302|1=hōren}}. However, during the subsequent 13 years leading up to the {{ill|lt=Kohei|Q1193572|WD=Q1193572|1=Kōhei}} year, wars such as {{ill|lt=Zenunen no Eki|Q1187448|WD=Q1187448|1=Former Nine Years' War}} occurred, and the shrine grounds were confiscated and fell into disrepair, with only a small shrine erected. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Gohorikawa|Q357221|WD=Q357221|1=Emperor Go-Horikawa}}, a shrine was built in the village at the request of parishioners, and the object of worship was moved from Dorikushinzan to its current enshrined place in {{ill|lt=Hiroki|Q1194200|WD=Q1194200|1=Kangi}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1230|Q5412|WD=Q5412|1=1230}}) {{ill|lt=April 8th|Q2503|WD=Q2503|1=April 8}}. After that, at the request of the parishioners, {{ill|lt=Kenno Shrine|Q11616108|WD=Q11616108|1=Kusano Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine in Iitate Village, which is a branch shrine of Kakeno Shrine in Namie Town) (Iitate Village|UNKNOWN|lt=Watatsumi Shrine}}), {{ill|lt=Yamyojin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Yamiyoshikami|1=Kuraokami}}, called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'', was enshrined together as a side shrine. For this reason, the company name was changed to Hachiryu Shrine, and it came to be called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'' by the people of the county. Since then, it has been dedicated to three deities, and rituals such as riding down the beach have also started to be held. According to the description, a mikoshi is carried out for the Hamakudari ritual on {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}}, and is enshrined at Kumaki Akira Shrine in the Kadokawara area at night. It is said that in the morning of the next morning, people descended on the beach in the present-day Minami-ebi area to pray for peace and peace in the world, long life for the military, abundant harvests, and safety for all people.Sukuhikona no Mikoto, one of the current deities enshrined, was discovered according to a Shikinai shrine survey in the county in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}}2 ({{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}}). Hachiryu Shrine is thought to be the Ronsha of Kanmine Shrine, and after the name was changed to Kanmine Shrine in 1825 (Bunsei 8), it is thought to have been enshrined together with Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward. === Precincts === * Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, main hall) * In addition, there are stone shrines and precinct shrines located within the precincts. * The {{ill|lt=Chinju-no-Mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158|1=Chinju no Mori}} within the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Minamisoma City as the ``Kanmine Shrine Forest''{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Kashima's Cultural Properties|url=http://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunnkazai/b-kashima/kabun.jsp|work= |publisher=Minamisoma City|date= |accessdate=2012-11-09}}. File:Torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine File:Small shrines of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts {{-}} === Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma City|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679|1=Haramachi-ku}}194 Shinoda Sawatonouchi |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Founded = unknown |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |alias= |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April 17th|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukahikona no Mikoto}} is enshrined here. === History === Its founding is unknown, but it is said to have declined with the passage of time, and only a small shrine remained. Furthermore, the shrine building was destroyed by a wild fire in ancient times, and after that a shrine was built to enshrine it. {{ill|lt=Hoei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} from {{ill|lt=Mashinori (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Mashinori|1=Shōtoku (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}-{{ill|lt=1715|Q6884|WD=Q6884|1=1715}}) When the family of Kudayu Ota, who was a shrine official, died out, Sankaku-in, a Haguro-ha Shugen, performed the rituals in their place. It is said that the number of residents decreased from the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era, and by the Bunka era, both the village and the shrine were in ruins. It is said that a local farmer moved the object of worship to a small shrine in a private house, hid it, and for many years it was enshrined by a graduate school of Honzan-ha Shugen Shugen. As time passed and these farmers disappeared, the fact that Kanmine no Kami was once enshrined in a small shrine in the village was forgotten.A man called the Sato clan came from another village and began living in the mansion of a farmer who was worshiping the Kanmine deity. While Mr. Sato was engaged in farming, he repaired and worshiped a small shrine on the mountain of Sakamine. {{ill|lt=Culture (Era name)|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Culture|1=Bunka}}12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|WD=Q6948|1=1815}}) Year of the Pig, {{jalink|Soma Nakamura Domain|Q844079|Ao {{ill|lt=Masutane Souma|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605|1=Sōma Masutane}}, the lord of the horse clan, ordered the shrine priest and a person named Mitsuna Watabe to investigate and identify eight Shikinai shrines in Namegata district. As a result, {{ill|lt=Taka Shrine|Q11430895|WD=Q11430895|1=Taka Shrine (Minamisōma)}}, {{ill|lt=Kashima Miko Shrine|Q11677124|WD=Q11677124|1=Kashima Miko Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Mito Shrine|Q11488802|WD=Q11488802|1=Mito Shrine}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshio Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Koza Shrine|Q11669904|WD=Q11669904|1=Takakura Shrine}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hisatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} were identified, but Kanmine Shrine and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masutamine Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} could not be identified because it was unclear which shrines they were referring to. {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}}2 ({{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}}) In the year of the Rabbit, another investigation order is given. The county official heard that there was a small shrine dedicated to the god of Kanmine in the village and investigated, but the villagers, who became suspicious, denied the existence of Kanmine Shrine, and as a result, Kanmine Shrine was even more forgotten from history.In the {{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|WD=Q1327259|1=Ansei}} years, thanks to {{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|WD=Q1327259|1=Ansei}}, the financial reconstruction of the Soma clan and the reconstruction of devastated farming villages progressed thanks to the ``Kokoku Anminho'', an agricultural reconstruction measure brought to Soma by {{ill|lt=Sontoku Ninomiya|Q968964|WD=Q968964|1=Ninomiya Sontoku}} and the Ninomiya family. {{ill|lt=Mannobu|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862|1=Man'en}} In the spring of the first year of the Koshin year ({{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691|1=1860}}), the restoration of the shrine shrine of Kanmine Shrine was ordered, and master craftsman Uesugi Shudono, a master craftsman from Aizu, constructed the shrine. In addition to the two Kanmine Shrines in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, and Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Keisoku Shrine, located in Kitaebi, Kashima Ward, also called itself Kanmine Shrine, but Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward It has been called Kanmine Shrine since ancient times, and the {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}} is said to have a history of more than a thousand years, so our company was considered to be the headquarters of Kanmine Shrine. File:Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine at Kashima ward,Minami-soma city.JPG|Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine (Kitaebi, Kashima-ku, Minami-soma city) === Precincts === * The shrine building is located on the hill on the west side of {{ill|lt=Tokiwa Expressway|Q1059875|WD=Q1059875|1=Jōban Expressway}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma Interchange|Q10908775|WD=Q10908775|1=Minamisoma interchange}}. * At the entrance to the shrine is the first torii gate with a plaque reading ``Kanreisha.'' From there, climb up the stone steps for about 200 meters to reach the second torii gate of Kanmine Shrine. File:View of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Distant view of Kanmine-jinja shrine enshrined place File:1st torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine First Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach entrance File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Sando File:2nd torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine second torii gate File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Sando File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Front part of the shrine File:Carving of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Carving of the worship hall
There are carvings of rainbow beams, toaded dragons, phoenixes, plants, wood-nosed lions, etc. File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine main hall === Source === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Oku Soshi (Soma City History 4) Soma City 1969 * Engishiki Kanmine Shrine abbreviation All parishioners April 1994 * Kanmine Shrine, one of the eight shrines Haramachi City Board of Education {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakamine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma City|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}. {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}} It is one of eight troupes located in {{ill|lt=Namegata District (Fukushima Prefecture)|Q11626139|WD=Q11626139|lt=Namegata District|1=Namekata district}}. Two shrines located in Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, and Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward are said to be the main nonsha. === Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|270px]]
Haden of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma City|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}{{ill|lt=Kashima Ward|Q24895757|WD=Q24895757|1=Kashima-ku}}Kamitochikubo Miyashita 35 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto}}

{{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto|1=Hoori}}
(den) {{ill|lt=Yamikamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Yamitakamikami|1=Kuraokami}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Also known as Hachiryu Gongen/Hachiryu Daimyojin |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April 8th|Q2503|WD=Q2503|1=April 8}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|43|27.7|N|140|52|32.4|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * According to the history book in the precincts, {{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto|1=Hoori}} are enshrined here. ** According to the Shikinai-sha Kensho-kai, the current enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhiko Hibunigei no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto. ** According to ``Okusōshi'', it is said to enshrine the three shrines of Amatsuhiko Hinaunigei no Mikoto, Amatsuhiko Hikoidemi no Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Yokamijin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Hachiryu Daimyojin|1=Kuraokami}}. * Both the {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|lt=Goshintai|1=Shintai}} of {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemige no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Amatsuhiko Hidemi no Mikoto|1=Hoori}} are said to be "statues of horsemen." === History === According to historical records, during the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nippon Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} came to Oshu after receiving an imperial order to conquer Toi. At that time, they received a spiritual message from {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}, the landowner's god, and they solicited the Emperor's grandson (Amatsuhiko-Kainonige-no-Mikoto) and performed rituals for 17 days. The mountain where he camped was surrounded by rivers on all sides, and it is said that Nippon Takeru was able to take advantage of the geographical advantage and defeat the bandits and successfully pacify the area. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto, a landowner deity, was also known as Dosojin and Dorikujin. It is said that the mountain where Kanmine Shrine was built was named ``Dorokujinzan'' (currently Dorokujinzan) because it was the mountain where the god Dorikami had miraculous experiences. At the southern foot of Mt. Dorikujinzan, a waterhole called ``Mano Pond'' was established with water from Amano Manai and was used as a wash basin for Kanminejin. The name Mano River is said to have come from this ``Mano Pond.'' Additionally, there is a strange rock called ``Tateishi'' upstream of the Mano River, which is said to be the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto descended and was enshrined.During the {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193549|WD=Q1193549|1=Eishō (Heian period)}} era, which was the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Reizei|Q374125|WD=Q374125|1=Emperor Reizei}}, a ceremony was held to pray for peace in the land by holding a {{ill|lt=Hotori|Q11675302|WD=Q11675302|1=hōren}}. However, during the subsequent 13 years leading up to the {{ill|lt=Kohei|Q1193572|WD=Q1193572|1=Kōhei}} year, wars such as {{ill|lt=Zenunen no Eki|Q1187448|WD=Q1187448|1=Former Nine Years' War}} occurred, and the shrine grounds were confiscated and fell into disrepair, with only a small shrine erected. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Gohorikawa|Q357221|WD=Q357221|1=Emperor Go-Horikawa}}, a shrine was built in the village at the request of parishioners, and the object of worship was moved from Dorikushinzan to its current enshrined place in {{ill|lt=Hiroki|Q1194200|WD=Q1194200|1=Kangi}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1230|Q5412|WD=Q5412|1=1230}}) {{ill|lt=April 8th|Q2503|WD=Q2503|1=April 8}}. After that, at the request of the parishioners, {{ill|lt=Kenno Shrine|Q11616108|WD=Q11616108|1=Kusano Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine in Iitate Village, which is a branch shrine of Kakeno Shrine in Namie Town) (Iitate Village|UNKNOWN|lt=Watatsumi Shrine}}), {{ill|lt=Yamyojin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Yamiyoshikami|1=Kuraokami}}, called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'', was enshrined together as a side shrine. For this reason, the company name was changed to Hachiryu Shrine, and it came to be called ``Hachiryu Daimyojin'' by the people of the county. Since then, it has been dedicated to three deities, and rituals such as riding down the beach have also started to be held. According to the description, a mikoshi is carried out for the Hamakudari ritual on {{ill|lt=July 7|Q2694|WD=Q2694|1=July 7}}, and is enshrined at Kumaki Akira Shrine in the Kadokawara area at night. It is said that in the morning of the next morning, people descended on the beach in the present-day Minami-ebi area to pray for peace and peace in the world, long life for the military, abundant harvests, and safety for all people.Sukuhikona no Mikoto, one of the current deities enshrined, was discovered according to a Shikinai shrine survey in the county in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}}2 ({{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}}). Hachiryu Shrine is thought to be the Ronsha of Kanmine Shrine, and after the name was changed to Kanmine Shrine in 1825 (Bunsei 8), it is thought to have been enshrined together with Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward. === Precincts === * Shrine building (haiden, shrine hall, main hall) * In addition, there are stone shrines and precinct shrines located within the precincts. * The {{ill|lt=Chinju-no-Mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158|1=Chinju no Mori}} within the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Minamisoma City as the ``Kanmine Shrine Forest''{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Kashima's Cultural Properties|url=http://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunnkazai/b-kashima/kabun.jsp|work= |publisher=Minamisoma City|date= |accessdate=2012-11-09}}. File:Torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine File:Small shrines of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Kashima ward.JPG|Stone shrine in the precincts {{-}} === Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, Minamisoma City) === {{Shrine |Name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward) |Image=[[File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma City|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679|1=Haramachi-ku}}194 Shinoda Sawatonouchi |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|name=Kanmine Shrine (Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Founded = unknown |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |alias= |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=April 17th|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Kanmine Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|37|39|25.7|N|140|54|59.1|E}}}} }} === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Sukunahikona|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukahikona no Mikoto}} is enshrined here. === History === Its founding is unknown, but it is said to have declined with the passage of time, and only a small shrine remained. Furthermore, the shrine building was destroyed by a wild fire in ancient times, and after that a shrine was built to enshrine it. {{ill|lt=Hoei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} from {{ill|lt=Mashinori (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Mashinori|1=Shōtoku (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}-{{ill|lt=1715|Q6884|WD=Q6884|1=1715}}) When the family of Kudayu Ota, who was a shrine official, died out, Sankaku-in, a Haguro-ha Shugen, performed the rituals in their place. It is said that the number of residents decreased from the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era, and by the Bunka era, both the village and the shrine were in ruins. It is said that a local farmer moved the object of worship to a small shrine in a private house, hid it, and for many years it was enshrined by a graduate school of Honzan-ha Shugen Shugen. As time passed and these farmers disappeared, the fact that Kanmine no Kami was once enshrined in a small shrine in the village was forgotten.A man called the Sato clan came from another village and began living in the mansion of a farmer who was worshiping the Kanmine deity. While Mr. Sato was engaged in farming, he repaired and worshiped a small shrine on the mountain of Sakamine. {{ill|lt=Culture (Era name)|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Culture|1=Bunka}}12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|WD=Q6948|1=1815}}) Year of the Pig, {{jalink|Soma Nakamura Domain|Q844079|Ao {{ill|lt=Masutane Souma|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605|1=Sōma Masutane}}, the lord of the horse clan, ordered the shrine priest and a person named Mitsuna Watabe to investigate and identify eight Shikinai shrines in Namegata district. As a result, {{ill|lt=Taka Shrine|Q11430895|WD=Q11430895|1=Taka Shrine (Minamisōma)}}, {{ill|lt=Kashima Miko Shrine|Q11677124|WD=Q11677124|1=Kashima Miko Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Mito Shrine|Q11488802|WD=Q11488802|1=Mito Shrine}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshio Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Koza Shrine|Q11669904|WD=Q11669904|1=Takakura Shrine}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hisatsu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} were identified, but Kanmine Shrine and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masutamine Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} could not be identified because it was unclear which shrines they were referring to. {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}}2 ({{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}}) In the year of the Rabbit, another investigation order is given. The county official heard that there was a small shrine dedicated to the god of Kanmine in the village and investigated, but the villagers, who became suspicious, denied the existence of Kanmine Shrine, and as a result, Kanmine Shrine was even more forgotten from history.In the {{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|WD=Q1327259|1=Ansei}} years, thanks to {{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|WD=Q1327259|1=Ansei}}, the financial reconstruction of the Soma clan and the reconstruction of devastated farming villages progressed thanks to the ``Kokoku Anminho'', an agricultural reconstruction measure brought to Soma by {{ill|lt=Sontoku Ninomiya|Q968964|WD=Q968964|1=Ninomiya Sontoku}} and the Ninomiya family. {{ill|lt=Mannobu|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862|1=Man'en}} In the spring of the first year of the Koshin year ({{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691|1=1860}}), the restoration of the shrine shrine of Kanmine Shrine was ordered, and master craftsman Uesugi Shudono, a master craftsman from Aizu, constructed the shrine. In addition to the two Kanmine Shrines in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward, and Kamitochikubo, Kashima Ward, Keisoku Shrine, located in Kitaebi, Kashima Ward, also called itself Kanmine Shrine, but Kanmine Shrine in Shindazawa, Haramachi Ward It has been called Kanmine Shrine since ancient times, and the {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}} is said to have a history of more than a thousand years, so our company was considered to be the headquarters of Kanmine Shrine. File:Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine at Kashima ward,Minami-soma city.JPG|Haiden of Keisoku-jinja shrine (Kitaebi, Kashima-ku, Minami-soma city) === Precincts === * The shrine building is located on the hill on the west side of {{ill|lt=Tokiwa Expressway|Q1059875|WD=Q1059875|1=Jōban Expressway}}{{ill|lt=Minamisoma Interchange|Q10908775|WD=Q10908775|1=Minamisoma interchange}}. * At the entrance to the shrine is the first torii gate with a plaque reading ``Kanreisha.'' From there, climb up the stone steps for about 200 meters to reach the second torii gate of Kanmine Shrine. File:View of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Distant view of Kanmine-jinja shrine enshrined place File:1st torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine First Torii File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Kanmine Shrine approach entrance File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Sando File:2nd torii gate of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Kanmine Shrine second torii gate File:Main approach to Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Sando File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Kanmine Shrine worship hall File:Haiden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 3.JPG|Front part of the shrine File:Carving of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Carving of the worship hall
There are carvings of rainbow beams, toaded dragons, phoenixes, plants, wood-nosed lions, etc. File:Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Honden of Sakamine-jinja shrine main hall === Source === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Oku Soshi (Soma City History 4) Soma City 1969 * Engishiki Kanmine Shrine abbreviation All parishioners April 1994 * Kanmine Shrine, one of the eight shrines Haramachi City Board of Education {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakamine Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakamine Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]
